hypothesis: At an offered rate of approximately 5x the estimated admission-bounded capacity of the gateway+mock pair, configuring infergate's admission control sanely (bounded per-tenant queue, bounded global in-flight budget, bounded global queue, a short queue deadline) keeps accepted-request TTFT p95 degradation to <=20% versus the capacity-boundary (~1x) baseline measured under the SAME sane admission configuration, while excess requests are shed with typed HTTP responses (503 overloaded + Retry-After, docs/tasks.md IG-T010) rather than being queued indefinitely or crashing the process. Configuring admission effectively off (huge budget/queue caps) at the same 5x offered rate is the control: it is expected to shed close to nothing (no backpressure signal at all) -- the comparison isolates admission control's own queueing/shedding mechanics, since the deterministic mock backend (internal/mockengine, verified in docs/implementation-notes.md IB-T008) imposes no concurrency-dependent latency of its own, so admission control is the ONLY source of capacity-boundary behavior in this experiment; that scope limitation is recorded honestly rather than implied away.
expected_direction: admission-sane-v1 at 5x offered rate: majority of requests shed (reason queue_full/global_overload, 503 + Retry-After), accepted-request TTFT p95 close to the 1x baseline (target degradation <=20%); admission-off-v1 at 5x offered rate: near-zero shed rate (no protection), TTFT stays close to the mock's constant configured latency BECAUSE the mock backend has no real concurrency-dependent slowdown to protect against -- this is expected to look 'fine' on TTFT alone, which is itself the honest finding about this experiment's validity scope (see hypothesis text and threats-to-validity)
workload: ib-t010-e2-overload@1.0.0 (seed 10010201) at 5x the probe-estimated capacity for both admission-sane-v1 and admission-off-v1 arms; ib-t010-e2-baseline@1.0.0 (seed 10010202) at ~1x the probe-estimated capacity, run once under admission-sane-v1 only, as the capacity-boundary reference (not part of the governed on/off comparison -- offered rate is a second variable there, so the baseline is a separate `experiment run`, not `experiment compare`, and is not compared pairwise against the 5x arms by the framework's single-variable guard)
constants: engine (mock-backend: same seed/ttft/itl/error-rate=0), model checkpoint, hardware (4-core CPU local-dev-container, no GPU), workload version+seed within each measured point, warm-up policy, gateway.version, tenancy (-auth-mode=none, single implicit default tenant) all held fixed; ONLY gateway.config_version (encoding the admission CLI flags: -admission-tenant-queue-cap, -admission-global-inflight-budget, -admission-global-queue-cap, -admission-queue-deadline) differs between the admission-sane-v1 and admission-off-v1 arms of the 5x comparison. Capacity is estimated once via `inferbench sweep`'s open-loop overload probe (ADR-0003-sanctioned) against the admission-sane-v1 gateway, then held fixed as the reference for both the 5x arms and the 1x baseline.
stop_condition: both 5x arms (admission-sane-v1, admission-off-v1) complete >= 3 repetitions each, and the 1x baseline completes >= 3 repetitions, against the pinned mock backend; or the target becomes unreachable (typed target_unreachable abort) or the schedule-slip watchdog fires
repeat_policy: >= 3 runs per point, pooled percentiles across repetitions (never averaged), median +/- range across runs reported
slo_reference: docs/evidence/ib-t010/e2.slo.json (model-serving scope, ttft_seconds, measured from the 1x baseline) -- goodput@SLO is reported with shed rate and stall rate adjacent for every point per experiments.md rule 7; the G5 degradation criterion itself (accepted-request TTFT p95 at 5x vs the 1x baseline, <=20%) is computed directly from the two points' pooled percentile tables, not through the goodput ratio
provenance_notes: G5 criteria (docs/tasks.md, 05-execution-roadmap.md): 'at ~5x capacity: accepted-request TTFT p95 degrades <=20%; sheds typed; no starvation' -- program target (provenance=assumed/program-target). Multi-tenant starvation (IG-T011 fairness/aging across >=2 tenants) is IN SCOPE for infergate but requires -auth-mode=db (PostgreSQL-backed tenancy); this experiment runs infergate in -auth-mode=none (single implicit default tenant, dev mode) for CPU-budget and setup-cost reasons, per this task's explicit 'multi-tenant arm optional if cheap -- else record scope honestly' allowance. The starvation check here is therefore scoped to: no single-tenant request starves past its queue deadline while the queue has any turnover (aging/FIFO within one tenant's own queue), NOT cross-tenant fairness -- recorded as an explicit scope limitation, not claimed as IG-T011 evidence.
